question_text: Let 𝑋1, 𝑋2, … , 𝑋𝑛 be a random sample of size 𝑛 (≥2) from a population having probability density function 𝑓(𝑥; 𝜃) = { 2 𝜃𝑥(−log𝑒𝑥)𝑒− (log𝑒𝑥)2 𝑖𝑓 0 < 𝑥< 1 0 𝑜𝑡ℎ𝑒𝑟𝑤𝑖𝑠𝑒, where 𝜃> 0 is an unknown parameter. Then which one of the following statements is true?

options.A: (log𝑒𝑋𝑖)2 𝑖=1 is the maximum likelihood estimator of 𝜃
options.B: 𝑛−1 ∑ (log𝑒𝑋𝑖)2 𝑖=1 is the maximum likelihood estimator of 𝜃
options.C: log𝑒𝑋𝑖 𝑖=1 is the maximum likelihood estimator of 𝜃
options.D: 𝑛−1 ∑ log𝑒𝑋𝑖 𝑖=1 is the maximum likelihood estimator of 𝜃
